summary:
The available literature does not indicate any significant difference in deflation rates between prefilled and standard textured saline breast implants; both types rely on similar mechanisms for filling and leak detection. Given limited studies comparing these two forms directly, it cannot be concluded that one has higher risk than another regarding deflation outcomes.

Current evidence lacks sufficient data from head-to-head studies to confirm any disparity in their performance concerning leakage or volume loss over time.
